title: Switch from manual frequency encodings to CatBoost ordered CTRs on high-cardinality interactions
summary: The current best solutions still handcraft categorical summaries.
rationale: The current best solutions still handcraft categorical summaries. CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered target statistics were designed to reduce prediction shift and leakage for sparse/high-cardinality categoricals, which fits `Driver`, `Race`, `Race_Year`, compound transitions, and their interactions better than static frequency features.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost classifier on mostly raw columns plus a few robust numerics, letting it form ordered CTRs over combos such as `Driver x Race_Year`, `Driver x Compound`, `Race_Year x Stint`, `Driver x Stint x Compound`. Compare against the current LightGBM/XGBoost-style baseline and keep it as either a replacement or ensemble member.
expected_effect: Best chance of capturing driver- and circuit-specific pit priors without fragile manual target encoding.
risk: If folds are not strictly temporal/group-safe, CatBoost can still look better offline than online; tuning can also be slower than the current baseline.
